title: vicia monantha subsp. triflora
intro: Vicia monantha subsp. triflora, also known as the three-flowered vetch, is a subspecies of perennial vetch native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its trifoliate leaves and small white or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Vicia monantha subsp. triflora include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe digestive issues, due to its high content of alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be dried and used as a powder or in capsules for its medicinal properties.
